requirements.summary: The character data in a UTF-8 Encoded String MUST be well-formed UTF-8 as defined by the Unicode specification and restated in RFC 3629 . In particular, the character data MUST NOT include encodings of code points between U+D800 and U+DFFF .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to core packet framing and codec modules.

requirements.summary: A UTF-8 Encoded String MUST NOT include an encoding of the null character U+0000. . If a receiver (Server or Client) receives an MQTT Control Packet containing U+0000 it is a Malformed Packet. Refer to section 4.13 for information about handling errors.
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to core packet framing and codec modules.

requirements.summary: A UTF-8 encoded sequence 0xEF 0xBB 0xBF is always interpreted as U+FEFF ("ZERO WIDTH NO-BREAK SPACE") wherever it appears in a string and MUST NOT be skipped over or stripped off by a packet receiver .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to core packet framing and codec modules.

requirements.summary: Both strings MUST comply with the requirements for UTF-8 Encoded Strings . If a receiver (Client or Server) receives a string pair which does not meet these requirements it is a Malformed Packet. Refer to section 4.13 for information about handling errors.

requirements.summary: The remaining bits of byte 1 in the Fixed Header contain flags specific to each MQTT Control Packet type as shown below. Where a flag bit is marked as “Reserved”, it is reserved for future use and MUST be set to the value listed .

requirements.summary: A PUBACK, PUBREC , PUBREL, or PUBCOMP packet MUST contain the same Packet Identifier as the PUBLISH packet that was originally sent . A SUBACK and UNSUBACK MUST contain the Packet Identifier that was used in the corresponding SUBSCRIBE and UNSUBSCRIBE packet respectively .

requirements.summary: The Keep Alive is a Two Byte Integer which is a time interval measured in seconds. It is the maximum time interval that is permitted to elapse between the point at which the Client finishes transmitting one MQTT Control Packet and the point it starts sending the next.

requirements.summary: When a Server takes ownership of an incoming Application Message it MUST add it to the Session State for those Clients that have matching Subscriptions . Matching rules are defined in section 4.7.
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: Under normal circumstances Clients receive messages in response to Subscriptions they have created. A Client could also receive messages that do not match any of its explicit Subscriptions. This can happen if the Server automatically assigned a subscription to the Client.
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: When the Client re-sends any PUBLISH packets, it MUST re-send them in the order in which the original PUBLISH packets were sent (this applies to QoS 1 and QoS 2 messages)
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: The Client MUST send PUBACK packets in the order in which the corresponding PUBLISH packets were received (QoS 1 messages)
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: The Client MUST send PUBREC packets in the order in which the corresponding PUBLISH packets were received (QoS 2 messages)
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: The Client MUST send PUBREL packets in the order in which the corresponding PUBREC packets were received (QoS 2 messages)
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: An Ordered Topic is a Topic where the Client can be certain that the Application Messages in that Topic from the same Client and at the same QoS are received are in the order they were published. When a Server processes a message that has been published to an Ordered Topic, it...
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: By default, a Server MUST treat every Topic as an Ordered Topic when it is forwarding messages on Non‑shared Subscriptions. . A Server MAY provide an administrative or other mechanism to allow one or more Topics to not be treated as an Ordered Topic.
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: The wildcard characters can be used in Topic Filters, but MUST NOT be used within a Topic Name .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: The number sign (‘#’ U+0023) is a wildcard character that matches any number of levels within a topic. The multi-level wildcard represents the parent and any number of child levels. The multi-level wildcard character MUST be specified either on its own or following a topic lev...
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: The single-level wildcard can be used at any level in the Topic Filter, including first and last levels. Where it is used, it MUST occupy an entire level of the filter . It can be used at more than one level in the Topic Filter and can be used in conjunction with the multi-lev...
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: The Server MUST NOT match Topic Filters starting with a wildcard character (# or +) with Topic Names beginning with a $ character . The Server SHOULD prevent Clients from using such Topic Names to exchange messages with other Clients.
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: · All Topic Names and Topic Filters MUST be at least one character long
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: · Topic Names and Topic Filters MUST NOT include the null character (Unicode U+0000)
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: · Topic Names and Topic Filters are UTF-8 Encoded Strings; they MUST NOT encode to more than 65,535 bytes . Refer to section 1.5.4.
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: When it performs subscription matching the Server MUST NOT perform any normalization of Topic Names or Topic Filters, or any modification or substitution of unrecognized characters . Each non-wildcarded level in the Topic Filter has to match the corresponding level in the Topi...
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: A Shared Subscription's Topic Filter MUST start with $share/ and MUST contain a ShareName that is at least one character long . The ShareName MUST NOT contain the characters "/", "+" or "#", but MUST be followed by a "/" character.
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: A Shared Subscription's Topic Filter MUST start with $share/ and MUST contain a ShareName that is at least one character long . The ShareName MUST NOT contain the characters "/", "+" or "#", but MUST be followed by a "/" character.
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: · Different subscribing Clients are permitted to ask for different Requested QoS levels in their SUBSCRIBE packets. The Server decides which Maximum QoS to grant to each Client, and it is permitted to grant different Maximum QoS levels to different subscribers.
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: · If the Server is in the process of sending a QoS 2 message to its chosen subscribing Client and the connection to the Client breaks before delivery is complete, the Server MUST complete the delivery of the message to that Client when it reconnects as described in section 4.3.3.
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: · If the Server is in the process of sending a QoS 2 message to its chosen subscribing Client and the connection to the Client breaks before delivery is complete, the Server MUST complete the delivery of the message to that Client when it reconnects as described in section 4.3.3.
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: · If a Client responds with a PUBACK or PUBREC containing a Reason Code of 0x80 or greater to a PUBLISH packet from the Server, the Server MUST discard the Application Message and not attempt to send it to any other Subscriber .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: The Client or Server MUST set its initial send quota to a non-zero value not exceeding the Receive Maximum .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: Each time the Client or Server sends a PUBLISH packet at QoS > 0, it decrements the send quota. If the send quota reaches zero, the Client or Server MUST NOT send any more PUBLISH packets with QoS > 0 . It MAY continue to send PUBLISH packets with QoS 0, or it MAY choose to su...
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: Each time the Client or Server sends a PUBLISH packet at QoS > 0, it decrements the send quota. If the send quota reaches zero, the Client or Server MUST NOT send any more PUBLISH packets with QoS > 0 . It MAY continue to send PUBLISH packets with QoS 0, or it MAY choose to su...
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: To begin an enhanced authentication, the Client includes an Authentication Method in the CONNECT packet. This specifies the authentication method to use. If the Server does not support the Authentication Method supplied by the Client, it MAY send a CONNACK with a Reason Code o...
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: If the Server requires additional information to complete the authentication, it can send an AUTH packet to the Client. This packet MUST contain a Reason Code of 0x18 (Continue authentication) . If the authentication method requires the Server to send authentication data to th...
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: The Client responds to an AUTH packet from the Server by sending a further AUTH packet. This packet MUST contain a Reason Code of 0x18 (Continue authentication) . If the authentication method requires the Client to send authentication data for the Server, it is sent in the Aut...
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: The Client can close the connection at any point in this process. It MAY send a DISCONNECT packet before doing so. The Server can reject the authentication at any point in this process. It MAY send a CONNACK with a Reason Code of 0x80 or above as described in section 4.13, and...
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: If the initial CONNECT packet included an Authentication Method property then all AUTH packets, and any successful CONNACK packet MUST include an Authentication Method Property with the same value as in the CONNECT packet .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: The implementation of enhanced authentication is OPTIONAL for both Clients and Servers. If the Client does not include an Authentication Method in the CONNECT, the Server MUST NOT send an AUTH packet, and it MUST NOT send an Authentication Method in the CONNACK packet .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: The implementation of enhanced authentication is OPTIONAL for both Clients and Servers. If the Client does not include an Authentication Method in the CONNECT, the Server MUST NOT send an AUTH packet, and it MUST NOT send an Authentication Method in the CONNACK packet .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: If the Client supplied an Authentication Method in the CONNECT packet it can initiate a re-authentication at any time after receiving a CONNACK. It does this by sending an AUTH packet with a Reason Code of 0x19 (Re-authentication).
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: If the re-authentication fails, the Client or Server SHOULD send DISCONNECT with an appropriate Reason Code as described in section 4.13, and MUST close the Network Connection .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: When a Server detects a Malformed Packet or Protocol Error, and a Reason Code is given in the specification, it MUST close the Network Connection . In the case of an error in a CONNECT packet it MAY send a CONNACK packet containing the Reason Code, before closing the Network C...
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: The CONNACK and DISCONNECT packets allow a Reason Code of 0x80 or greater to indicate that the Network Connection will be closed. If a Reason Code of 0x80 or greater is specified, then the Network Connection MUST be closed whether or not the CONNACK or DISCONNECT is sent .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.section_title: Using WebSocket as a network transport
requirements.summary: · MQTT Control Packets MUST be sent in WebSocket binary data frames. If any other type of data frame is received the recipient MUST close the Network Connection .
requirements.mapping_reason: Fallback mapping for section 6 (Using WebSocket as a network transport).

requirements.section_title: Using WebSocket as a network transport
requirements.summary: · A single WebSocket data frame can contain multiple or partial MQTT Control Packets. The receiver MUST NOT assume that MQTT Control Packets are aligned on WebSocket frame boundaries .
requirements.mapping_reason: Fallback mapping for section 6 (Using WebSocket as a network transport).

requirements.section_title: Using WebSocket as a network transport
requirements.summary: · The Client MUST include “mqtt” in the list of WebSocket Sub Protocols it offers .
requirements.mapping_reason: Fallback mapping for section 6 (Using WebSocket as a network transport).

requirements.section_title: Using WebSocket as a network transport
requirements.summary: · The WebSocket Subprotocol name selected and returned by the Server MUST be “mqtt” .
requirements.mapping_reason: Fallback mapping for section 6 (Using WebSocket as a network transport).
